item.title: About
item.description: Current Position I’m currently a Research Associate in the Integrative Biology department at the University of Texas at Austin, working with Dr. Lauren Ancel Meyers. Together, we run the UT COVID-19 Modeling Consortium, an interdisciplinary network of researchers and health professionals building models to detect, project, and combat COVID-19, where I’m currently serving as Associate Director. My Background I went to college at Rose-Hulman Insitute of Technology. After a short stint working towards a computer engineering degree, I switched to a biology, fell in love with research, and became fascinated with infectious diseases.

item.title: Media
item.description: COVID-19 Will update with highlights soon. Pandemic Flu Risk New york times - The Next Flu Pandemic Will Appear When You Least Expect It Austin local NPR. All things considered - October 20th, 2017 Austin360 - This year’s flu had nothing on the 1918 flu; UT researcher knows why UTNews - Cracking the Code: Why Flu Pandemics Come At the End of Flu Season The Daily Texan - UT researchers explain the timing of flu pandemics UT CNS - A Century After 1918 Flu, A Virus that Still Surprises Texas Health Journal - When the Flu Follows You Seasonal Flu KXAN News - UT researchers harnessing data to predict where flu could strike next in Texas HealthTech Magazine - Digital Data Makes Real-Time Flu Tracking a Reality Zika Risk in Texas The Texas Standard (Radio) - Begins at the 16 minute mark BMC Series blog - Assessing real-time Zika risk in the United States Austin American-Statesman - Risk of Zika epidemic in Texas is almost zero, except for these three places

item.title: Publications
item.description: I’m hoping to update this page soon, but please look at my google scholar page for my most updated list of publications and reports. Peer-Reviewed Publications Fox SJ, Miller JC, Meyers LA. (2017) Seasonality in Risk of Pandemic Influenza Emergence. PLOS Computational Biology 13(10): e1005749. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pcbi.1005749 Castro LA*, Fox SJ*, Chen X, Liu K, Bellan S, Dimitrov NB, Galvani AP, Meyers LA. Assessing real-time Zika risk in the United States.

item.title: Research Interests
item.description: My work focuses primarily on statistical modeling of infectious disease dynamics, and I’m interested in using these models to understand basic biological questions regarding disease spread and interventions (though I’ve recently worked on more applied projects with Zika). I’m especially interested in understanding multiple disease interactions on contact networks (e.g. influenza subtype competition, HIV/TB amplification, etc.), and have a few planned projects to continue in that realm for the future.
